titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Stephen Yenser Papers Relating to James Merrill
Yenser (Stephen) Papers Relating to James Merrill
titlestmt.author: by Andrea Benefiel and Jennifer Meehan

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10417382.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 04:09

did.unittitle: Stephen Yenser papers relating to James Merrill

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection consists of material created and accumlated by Stephen Yenser in the course of researching and editing the literary work of James Merrill. Material primarily includes correspondence between Yenser and Merrill spanning 1967 to 1995 and various drafts of Merrill's writings.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Stephen Yenser on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2002.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Stephen Yenser Papers Relating to James Merrill is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Stephen Yenser Papers Relating to James Merrill. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Stephen Yenser
bioghist.p: Stephen Yenser is an American poet and critic. He is the Distinguished Professor of English and Director of Creative Writing at the University of California, Los Angeles. In addition to publishing several books of poetry, Yenser authored The Consuming Myth: The Work of James Merrill (1987). Yenser has also co-edited a number of Merrill's works, including Collected Poems (2001), Collected Novels and Plays (2002), Collected Prose (2004), The Changing Light at Sandover (2006),and Selected Poems (2008).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of material created and accumlated by Stephen Yenser in the course of researching and editing the literary work of James Merrill. Material primarily includes correspondence between Yenser and Merrill spanning 1967 to 1995 and various drafts of Merrill's writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence, 1967-1991. II. Writings and Other Papers, 1970-2001.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. contains letters between Stephen Yenser and James Merrill and letters to Yenser from Peter Hooten.

did.unittitle: Merrill, James
Hooten, Peter
Writings and Other Papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Writings by James Merrill, Writings of Others, and Other Papers. Writings by James Merrill are arranged alphabetically by title, with untitled writings filed at the end of the subseries. Writings of Others are arranged alphabetically by author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings by James Merrill contain mostly typescript drafts of Merrill's writings, many containing manuscript corrections and notes. Also included are proofs, notebooks, and correspondence related to Merrill's works. Writings of Others include the libretto of Phaedra: An Opera in Two Acts , by Bernard de Zogheb, which contains a foreword written by Merrill; as well as photocopies of a poetic tribute to James Merrill, written by Rachel Hadas. Other Papers include a photograph of Merrill and a plate engraved with a swan.

did.unittitle: Writings by James Merrill
The Bait , typescript (photocopy), with letter to Yenser from Washington University Library dated 2001
"The Birthday," typescript (photocopy) and manuscript notes
Drafts of poems from the book, newspaper clipping, typescript, and typescript (photocopy), with corrections
Proofs (photocopy)
Coda: The Higher Keys , drafts, typescript (photocopy), with corrections
Yanina Notebook #1
Drafts, typescript, with corrections
Typescript pages
Typescript, bound in two volumes
Divine Comedies , drafts of poems, including The Book of Ephraim , autograph manuscript, typescript, and typescript (photocopy), with corrections
The Fire Season , drafts of poems from the book, typescript (photocopy), with corrections
The Image Maker , draft, typescript and typescript (photocopy), with corrections
The Inner Room , drafts, typescript and typescript (photocopy), with corrections
Last Poems , correspondence, corrected typescript and typescript (photocopy) drafts, proofs, and printed prospectus
The Late Settings , drafts, typescript and typescript (photocopy), with corrections
Mirabell's Books of Number , drafts, typescript and typescript (photocopy), with corrections and notes
"Rose," typescript (photocopy), 1949?
Drafts, typescript and typescript (photocopy), with corrections
Draft, typescript
Advance uncorrected proof
Scripts for the Pageant , drafts, typescript and typescript (photocopy), with corrections
Voices from Sandover , shooting script, typescript (photocopy), with corrections
"Yánnina, for Stephen Yenser," typescript drafts, with corrections
The Yellow Pages , typescript
Ouija Board Session, manuscript and typescript
Ouija Board Session, manuscript
Greek child's notebook, containing short verse by Merrill
Writings of Others
de Zogheb, Bernard. Phaedra: an opera in two acts , libretto, with forward by James Merrill
Hadas, Rachel. "Six Poems for James Merrill," printed version (photocopy)
Other Papers
Photograph of James Merrill by Tom Victor, inscribed to Stephen Yenser by Merrill
Plate with engraving of swan
[Wooden stick with a metal point that has a decorative metal casing, with the other end wrapped in rope]
